activities.instructions: Choose the correct present perfect form.
activities.prompt: I ___ this film three times.
activities.explanation: Con «I» usamos «have» y el participio irregular de «see» es «seen».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct present perfect form.
activities.prompt: Maya ___ her keys, so she cannot open the door.
activities.explanation: El sujeto singular «Maya» requiere «has lost»; el resultado sigue siendo relevante.

activities.instructions: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.
activities.prompt: Have you ___ travelled by ferry?
activities.explanation: «Ever» pregunta si la experiencia ha ocurrido alguna vez hasta ahora.

activities.instructions: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.
activities.prompt: We've ___ tried that restaurant, so we do not know what it is like.
activities.explanation: «Never» expresa ausencia de experiencia y no necesita otra negación.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct present perfect form.
activities.prompt: The parcel ___ arrived.
activities.explanation: Con sujeto singular usamos «has»; «just» va antes del participio «arrived».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct completion.
activities.prompt: Leo isn't hungry because he ___ lunch.
activities.explanation: «Has already eaten» expresa una acción anterior cuyo resultado explica la situación actual.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct completion.
activities.prompt: We haven't received the invoice ___.
activities.explanation: «Yet» aparece normalmente al final de una negativa para indicar «todavía».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct question.
activities.prompt: You want to know whether Priya has completed the report.

options.text: Has Priya finished the report yet?
Priya has finished the report yet?
Have Priya finish the report already?
Does Priya has finished the report?

activities.explanation: La pregunta se forma con «Has + sujeto + participio» y «yet» puede ir al final.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct participle.
activities.prompt: Our neighbours have ___ a solar battery.
activities.explanation: El participio irregular de «buy» es «bought».

activities.instructions: Choose the sentence that matches the situation.
activities.prompt: The floor is wet now because Ava finished cleaning it a moment ago.

options.text: Ava has just cleaned the floor.
Ava have just clean the floor.
Ava has just cleaning the floor.

activities.explanation: «Has just cleaned» conecta la acción muy reciente con el suelo mojado ahora.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct completion.
activities.prompt: This is the first time I ___ a presentation in English.
activities.explanation: Después de «This is the first time» usamos present perfect: «have given».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct completion.
activities.prompt: It is the most useful course she ___.
activities.explanation: Los superlativos suelen combinarse con «has ever + participio»: «has ever taken».

activities.instructions: Choose the option that fits an unfinished time period.
activities.prompt: It is still Wednesday morning. I ___ four customer calls this week.
activities.explanation: «This week» es un periodo todavía abierto y el sujeto «I» usa «have handled».

activities.instructions: Choose the option that explains the present result.
activities.prompt: The printer is working again because Sam ___ the paper blockage.
activities.explanation: «Has removed» presenta una acción terminada con un resultado visible ahora.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct response.
activities.prompt: “Has the technician called yet?”
activities.explanation: La respuesta breve repite el auxiliar: «No, she hasn't».

activities.instructions: Choose the option that best matches the meaning.
activities.prompt: Nora is in Helsinki now. She left for Helsinki this morning.
activities.explanation: «Has gone to» indica que Nora se ha marchado y todavía está allí.

activities.instructions: Choose the option that best matches the meaning.
activities.prompt: Owen visited Kyoto and returned home last month. We are discussing his travel experience, not its date.
activities.explanation: «Has been to» presenta una visita completada de la que la persona ha regresado.

activities.instructions: Choose the sentence with natural adverb placement.
activities.prompt: The team completed the safety checks earlier than expected.

options.text: The team has already completed the safety checks.
The team already has complete the safety checks.
The team has completed already yet the safety checks.
The team have completed the safety checks just already.

activities.explanation: «Already» se coloca de forma natural entre «has» y el participio «completed».

activities.instructions: Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct and logically fits.
activities.prompt: A colleague asks whether this is your first experience of remote teamwork.

options.text: I've never worked in a remote team before.
I haven't never worked in a remote team.
I've never work in a remote team before.
I never have worked yesterday in a remote team.

activities.explanation: «Never» ya expresa negación y va con «have worked» para una experiencia vital.

activities.instructions: Choose the completion that keeps the period open.
activities.prompt: The shop is still open today, and it ___ more than 200 orders so far.
activities.explanation: «So far» dentro de un día aún abierto pide «has processed».

activities.instructions: Choose the option that best expresses the current evidence.
activities.prompt: There is fresh paint on Kim's sleeve and the hallway walls now look different.

options.text: Kim has ever paint the hallway.
Kim have painted the hallway yesterday.

activities.explanation: «Has painted» presenta la acción completada a través de sus resultados visibles actuales.

activities.instructions: Choose the grammatically correct sentence.
activities.prompt: The speaker is surprised that the work is complete earlier than expected.
activities.explanation: En una pregunta de sorpresa, «already» puede aparecer al final de «Have you finished already?».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct completion.
activities.prompt: Neither of the two replacement parts ___ yet, so the repair cannot begin.
activities.explanation: «Neither» funciona aquí como sujeto singular y requiere «has arrived».

activities.instructions: Choose the sentence that correctly reports experience up to now.
activities.prompt: A reviewer is comparing every laptop they have tested during their career.

options.text: This is the lightest laptop I've ever tested.
This is the lightest laptop I've ever test.
This is the lighter laptop I ever have tested yesterday.
This is the lightest laptop I am ever testing.

activities.explanation: El superlativo «the lightest» se combina naturalmente con «I've ever tested».

activities.instructions: Choose the completion that best fits both clauses.
activities.prompt: We ___ every proposed venue so far, but we ___ a final decision yet.
activities.explanation: El sujeto «we» usa «have visited» y «haven't made»; «so far» y «yet» mantienen abierto el marco temporal.
